Caption: The AI-powered ultrasonics thermometry for assessing temperature distribution within the HIFU focal area in deep organs (a) A total of five New Zealand rabbits were selected for the animal experiments, and the HIFU imaging-treatment system was employed to acquire data. Iterative training and Knowledge Distillation were used for model training and inference. (b) The training process of temperature monitoring strategy. (c) SKL monition on temperature distribution within HIFU focal area in deep organ.

(a) Schematic diagram of the experimental system. It comprises a temperature detection system and a HIFU treatment system. The HIFU treatment system consists of a therapeutic imaging applicator, HIFU driving console and unit, ultrasound machine, and UI console. (b) Integrated therapeutic-imaging applicator. (c) Intubation of the rabbit, with the ventilator replacing the rabbit's spontaneous breathing. (d) Schematic position of the thermocouple insertion.

The framework of BMTS, which consists of three modules: RSP, MTKI, and SKL. The RSP module utilizes NCC coefficients, frequency domain filtering, and phase analysis algorithms to establish a mapping relationship between RF data and six respiratory stages. Next, the HIFU acoustic parameters were used as priors for the MTKI module, which employs the IN and SFF strategy, combined with a feature fusion algorithm, to capture the spatial feature maps of RF data. Finally, the SKL module decouples the association between x and the temperature in the HIFU focal area.
